class.name: GDKCaptureMetaData
class.brief_description: Script-side context for writing capture metadata.
class.description:
Holds a metadata write context created by [method GDKCapture.create_metadata]. PC GDK exposes metadata as process-wide [code]XAppCaptureMetadata*[/code] calls with no native handle, so this object gates intentional script writes rather than owning native memory. Use the [code]add_*_event[/code] methods to write one-shot events and the [code]start_*_state[/code] methods to write persistent states. Call [method stop_all_states] to end all active states. The context is automatically closed when the [GDKCaptureMetaData] object is freed; call [method close] to close it early.
All operations validate the context and event/state names before calling into the GDK. Operations on a closed or null context return a [GDKResult] with code [code]invalid_metadata_handle[/code]. Operations with an empty name return [code]invalid_metadata_name[/code].
PC GDK availability: all wrapped [code]XAppCaptureMetadata*[/code] functions are available in [code]_GAMING_DESKTOP[/code] builds via [code]XAppCapture.h[/code] and [code]xgameruntime.lib[/code].

method.name: is_valid
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when the script-side context is open and usable.

method.name: close
method.description: Close this script-side context. After [method close], [method is_valid] returns [code]false[/code]. Calling [method close] on an already-closed context is safe (no-op). The context is also closed automatically when the object is freed.

method.name: stop_all_states
method.description: Stop all active persistent states in the metadata write buffer. Wraps [code]XAppCaptureMetadataStopAllStates[/code]. Returns [GDKResult] with [code]invalid_metadata_handle[/code] if the context is not valid.

method.name: get_remaining_storage_bytes
method.description: Returns the number of bytes remaining in the local metadata write buffer. Wraps [code]XAppCaptureMetadataRemainingStorageBytesAvailable[/code]. Returns [code]-1[/code] when the context is invalid or the call fails.

method.name: add_string_event
method.description: Write a one-shot string event to the metadata buffer. [param priority] is a [enum Priority] value ([constant PRIORITY_GAMEPLAY] or [constant PRIORITY_IMPORTANT]). Wraps [code]XAppCaptureMetadataAddStringEvent[/code].

method.name: add_double_event
method.description: Write a one-shot double event to the metadata buffer. Wraps [code]XAppCaptureMetadataAddDoubleEvent[/code].

method.name: add_int32_event
method.description: Write a one-shot int32 event to the metadata buffer. The [param value] is clamped to a 32-bit signed integer range. Wraps [code]XAppCaptureMetadataAddInt32Event[/code].

method.name: start_string_state
method.description: Begin a persistent string state in the metadata buffer. The state remains active until [method stop_all_states] is called. Wraps [code]XAppCaptureMetadataStartStringState[/code].

method.name: start_double_state
method.description: Begin a persistent double state in the metadata buffer. The state remains active until [method stop_all_states] is called. Wraps [code]XAppCaptureMetadataStartDoubleState[/code].

method.name: start_int32_state
method.description: Begin a persistent int32 state in the metadata buffer. The [param value] is clamped to a 32-bit signed integer range. The state remains active until [method stop_all_states] is called. Wraps [code]XAppCaptureMetadataStartInt32State[/code].

constants.constant: Standard gameplay-level metadata priority. Maps to [code]XAppCaptureMetadataPriority::Informational[/code].
